subscriptions.title: Usage Reset Radar
subscriptions.description: Codex Reset Radar, RSS events, and the reset-time hook in one operational view.

subscriptions.imageCaptionDesc: Visual marker for the usage reset window.
subscriptions.currentWindowFallback: No current window status to display.
subscriptions.operatorBriefDesc: Condenses the external signal into status, alert posture, and subscription endpoints for dashboard use.
subscriptions.thirdPartyNotice: This module only consumes the third-party radar summary and source links. It sends account probes only when the window closes, which is treated as the Codex reset time, and it does not raise concurrency.
subscriptions.resetHookDesc: Only a new window-close signal triggers this hook. The backend clears stale cooldown and usage cache for all accounts, then runs a connection test to refresh status.

hookMessage.waiting_for_open_signal: Waiting for the window to close

subscriptions.recentEventsDesc: Shows only recent RSS titles, timestamps, and short summaries. Open the source link for full context.
subscriptions.feedsDesc: Original URLs are kept here for RSS readers, bots, or external monitors.

subscriptions.rssFeedDesc: Publishes only window open and close alerts, suitable for RSS readers or notification bots.
subscriptions.jsonStatusDesc: Machine-readable current status summary for scripts.
subscriptions.adminProxyDesc: This instance's authenticated admin summary with server-side caching.

accounts.quotaDistributionRefreshTitle: Trigger a usage probe across all eligible accounts, bypassing the snapshot cache
accounts.quotaDistributionRefreshStarted: Usage probe triggered; results will stream in shortly
accounts.recoveryDistributionDesc: {{recoverable}} / {{limited}} rate-limited accounts have a known recovery time.
accounts.recoveryDistributionEmpty: No accounts are currently rate-limited or quota-exhausted.
accounts.recoveryDistributionUnknownOnly: {{count}} rate-limited accounts do not have a known recovery time.
accounts.quotaResetDistributionDesc: {{known}} / {{total}} sampled accounts have a known reset time.
accounts.quotaResetSummaryDesc: {{count}} accounts are expected to reset in the next 7 days including today; known {{known}} / {{total}}.
accounts.quotaResetSummaryKnown: {{unknown}} accounts have no known reset time.
accounts.pressureForecastLogic: The supply pool covers every dispatchable account regardless of upstream type (OAuth + Responses API), filtered only by disabled / unauthorized / currently rate-limited. Burn forecasting still requires a quota window, so Responses API accounts and 5h-on-non-premium plans contribute capacity but skip burn projections. For each burn-eligible account we linearly extrapolate quota exhaustion from the current window usage ratio; accounts with elapsed < 5% of the window are excluded from ranking to avoid noise. Concurrency slot throughput is adaptive: 60s / avg_duration_ms (falls back to 6 rpm/slot, clamped to 1-30). Bulk-limit threshold is dispatchable − ceil(currentRpm / (avgConcurrency × rpm/slot)) — the minimum accounts required to absorb current RPM; falls back to 30% of samples when RPM = 0. Pool capacity is min(global RPM, dispatchable total concurrency × rpm/slot); event stream models both −1 exhaustion and +1 replenishment when accounts reset. RPM / future capacity ≥ 90% is high pressure, ≥ 100% is supply shortfall; the "soon" warning window is 20% of the active window (5h → 1h, 7d → 34h). The 429 signal is the fraction of dispatchable accounts that hit a 429 in the last hour, saturated at 30%. Acceleration factor = dominant + 0.5 × secondary (avoids double-counting correlated RPM/active signals), capped at ×2.5. If known samples cover < 40% we flag low confidence and avoid escalating risk from burn projections alone.
accounts.pressureForecastLowConfidenceHint: {{sampled}} accounts sampled, {{unknown}} unknown. Sample coverage is low so the predicted time is indicative only; real-time RPM/active signals are still reliable.

usage.title: Usage Statistics
usage.clearLogsDesc: All request logs will be removed immediately and the statistics view will refresh right away. This action cannot be undone.
usage.emptyDesc: Once requests pass through the proxy, recent logs and status codes will appear here.
usage.emptyFilteredDesc: No request logs match the current filters. Clear filters and try again.
usage.compactRequestTooltip: This request contains compacted conversation context
usage.modelStatsDesc: Aggregated by retained logs for requests, tokens, and amount.
usage.featureStatsDesc: Request traits grouped by codex2api proxy capabilities.
usage.endpointStatsDesc: Requests grouped by inbound API endpoint.
usage.apiKeyStatsDesc: Call volume grouped by API key.

promptFilter.title: Prompt Filter
promptFilter.description: Manage local prompt risk rules and review upstream cyber_policy hits.
promptFilter.loadingDesc: Syncing rule settings and hit logs.
promptFilter.keywordPlaceholder: Search preview, matches, error code, or key
promptFilter.rulesCatalogDesc: Rules shipped by the backend. Each built-in rule can be enabled or disabled.
promptFilter.ruleHelpTitle: How to Add a Well-Formed Rule
promptFilter.ruleHelpDesc: Custom rules are scored together with built-in rules.
promptFilter.ruleHelpBody1: Patterns use Go regular expressions. name must be unique; weight is the score added on match; strict marks a strong rule, and strict hits can trigger handling once the strict threshold is reached.
promptFilter.ruleHelpBody2: Use clear categories such as malware, reverse_engineering, or license_cracking. Give high-risk rules higher weight and validate them with the tester before relying on them.
promptFilter.customRulesDesc: Extra rules are stored in system settings and can be toggled or removed independently.

themeSettings.title: Theme Settings
themeSettings.description: Adjust the admin console appearance mode and visual style in one place.
themeSettings.modeLightDesc: Best for daylight, screen sharing, and bright environments.
themeSettings.modeDarkDesc: Best for low-light environments and long operations sessions.
themeSettings.previewDesc: The preview renders common console surfaces with the active theme variables.
themeSettings.stylesDesc: Each style updates the background, sidebar, cards, borders, and primary color together.

themeDesc.default: A stable blue accent for general admin use and default deployments.
themeDesc.claude: A warm paper-like palette with softer cool tones and clear hierarchy.
themeDesc.chatgpt: A clean neutral minimal style for high-frequency operation pages.
themeDesc.deepseek: A cooler technical style for dark consoles and monitoring workflows.
themeDesc.graphite: A neutral operations palette with cyan accents for key actions.
themeDesc.aurora: A crisp green-cyan style for lighter, brighter admin surfaces.
themeDesc.rose: A soft rose palette that keeps the console quiet and structured.
themeDesc.mono: A high-contrast grayscale style focused on text, borders, and density.
themeDesc.oneDarkPro: Atom-flavored editor palette with soft slate background and blue syntax accent.
themeDesc.githubDimmed: A softened GitHub palette tuned for long-session reading in the console.
themeDesc.tokyoNight: A purple-blue night-city palette with calm contrast and cool highlights.
themeDesc.dracula: An iconic violet-magenta editor palette with strong personality.
themeDesc.monokaiPro: A warm pink-on-charcoal classic editor palette with vivid accents.
themeDesc.nord: An arctic frost palette with cool blues and gentle contrast.
themeDesc.catppuccin: A pastel mauve palette balancing playfulness with operational clarity.
themeDesc.gruvbox: A warm retro amber palette inspired by film prints and terminals.
themeDesc.solarizedLight: Ethan Schoonover's iconic warm-yellow palette designed for eye comfort.
themeDesc.quietLight: VS Code's gentle low-saturation palette with soft purple accents.
themeDesc.ayuLight: A warm cream palette with vibrant orange accents from the Ayu family.
themeDesc.noctisLux: A warm cream palette with gold accents, the light face of the Noctis family.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage system configuration and runtime parameters.
settings.globalRpmDesc: Maximum requests per minute globally. 0 means unlimited.
settings.testModelDesc: Model name used for connection testing
settings.pgMaxConnsDesc: Requires container restart to take effect.
settings.redisPoolSizeDesc: Requires container restart to take effect.
settings.proxyUrlDesc: Default proxy URL for new accounts
settings.autoCleanUnauthorizedDesc: Checks every 30 seconds and removes accounts with unauthorized status.
settings.autoCleanRateLimitedDesc: Checks every 30 seconds and removes accounts with rate_limited status.
settings.autoCleanFullUsageDesc: Checks every 5 minutes and removes accounts with ≥ 100% usage. Accounts with active requests are skipped.
settings.autoCleanErrorDesc: Checks every 30 seconds and removes accounts in error state (e.g. expired refresh tokens).
settings.autoCleanExpiredDesc: Every 15 minutes, removes accounts older than 30 minutes regardless of usage. Runs immediately when toggled on.
settings.siteNameDesc: Shown in the sidebar, login page, and browser title so multiple deployments are easy to tell apart.
settings.siteLogoDesc: PNG, JPG, or SVG. Max 600KB. 80x80px square images are recommended; larger uploads are compressed proportionally.
settings.siteLogoInvalidType: Only PNG, JPG, or SVG files are supported
settings.siteLogoTooLarge: Could not compress this image below 600KB. Please choose a smaller image.
settings.siteLogoCompressionFailed: Automatic compression failed. Please choose a PNG, JPG, or SVG under 600KB.
settings.timezoneDesc: Controls date/time display in the frontend. The page will reload automatically after changing.
settings.showFullUsageNumbersDesc: When enabled, the Usage page shows full request and token counts. When disabled, it uses compact units like 1.2M and 3.4B.
settings.fastSchedulerEnabledDesc: Uses an in-memory fast scheduling algorithm, significantly reducing dispatch latency under high concurrency. Recommended for large account pools (100+).
settings.schedulerModeDesc: Round Robin distributes requests evenly across accounts. Remaining Quota prefers accounts with lower usage percentage.
settings.affinityModeDesc: Bounded (default): same session sticks to one account but auto-rotates after 50 requests, 5 minutes, or when the bound account becomes unhealthy. Off: every request re-picks via scheduler. Strict: legacy behavior, sticks long-term.
settings.allowRemoteMigrationDesc: When enabled, other codex2api instances can pull healthy accounts from this instance.
settings.allowRemoteMigrationRequiresSecret: Set an admin secret first. Remote migration cannot be enabled without admin authentication.
settings.resinDesc: When configured, all account requests are routed through Resin reverse proxy for sticky IP binding per account. Both fields must be set to enable; clear to disable.
settings.resinUrlDesc: Resin proxy pool address with token, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:2260/my-token
settings.resinPlatformNameDesc: Platform identifier in Resin, used to distinguish different services. e.g. codex2api
settings.adminSecretPlaceholder: Leave empty to disable admin auth
settings.adminSecretDesc: When set, all /api/admin/* endpoints require X-Admin-Key header. Leave empty to disable.
settings.adminSecretEnvOverride: ADMIN_SECRET is currently enabled in the environment, so admin login uses the environment value first.
settings.promptFilterEnabledDesc: System-level switch. Detailed rules live in the Prompt Filter page.
settings.promptFilterModeDesc: Monitor only records, warn passes through, block rejects requests.
settings.modelsDesc: The following models are currently available:
settings.deleteKeyDesc: After deletion, all clients using this key will immediately lose access. Please confirm.
settings.noKeysDesc: API requires no auth without keys. Generated keys will appear here.
settings.keyAuthNote: API requires no auth without keys. After adding the first key, all /v1/* requests need Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx
settings.maxRetriesRange: Retry account switches for transport errors, 5xx, and 401 (0~10), 0 = no retry
settings.maxRateLimitRetriesRange: Maximum account switches after a 429 rate limit (0~10), 0 = return the 429 directly
settings.testModelHint: Model used for account connection testing
settings.backgroundRefreshIntervalDesc: Controls how often RT refresh, usage probe batches, and recovery probes are triggered, in minutes (1~1440). Saving resets the timer to the new interval.
settings.usageProbeMaxAgeDesc: Requests the responses endpoint again only after the usage snapshot is older than this many minutes (1~10080).
settings.usageProbeConcurrencyDesc: Maximum parallel usage probes (1~128, default 16). Raising it speeds up sampling for large pools but may trigger upstream rate limits.
settings.recoveryProbeIntervalDesc: Minimum gap between recovery probes for banned accounts, in minutes (1~10080).
settings.lazyModeDesc: Disables proactive account refreshes and probes. RT accounts refresh AT only when dispatched; usage and rate-limit state update from real requests.
settings.runtimeOptimizationDesc: Control legacy Codex client compatibility, usage-log disk writes, stream flush cadence, and first-token timeout retries.
settings.clientCompatModeDesc: preserve is the default and keeps the client UA; auto upgrades only outdated codex_cli_rs UA; force always uses a server-generated UA.
settings.codexMinCliVersionDesc: codex_cli_rs UA below this version is replaced in auto mode, e.g. 0.118.0.
settings.usageLogModeDesc: full records all requests; errors records failures only; off disables usage_logs writes to reduce disk pressure.
settings.usageLogBatchSizeDesc: Write logs to the database once the buffer reaches this size (1~10000).
settings.usageLogFlushIntervalDesc: When the batch size is not reached, write at least once every N seconds (1~300).
settings.billingTierPolicyDesc: actual bills by upstream response.service_tier; requested preserves the old behavior and bills explicit fast/priority requests as Fast.
settings.streamFlushPolicyDesc: immediate flushes every SSE event; coalesce briefly batches events in memory to reduce syscall pressure at high RPM.
settings.streamFlushIntervalDesc: Minimum interval between flushes in coalesce mode, in milliseconds (1~1000).
settings.firstTokenTimeoutDesc: For streaming requests, cancel the current upstream call and retry with another account if no first meaningful event arrives within N seconds; 0 = disabled.
settings.imageStorageDesc: Choose where generated images are persisted: local disk or any S3-compatible object store (AWS S3 / Cloudflare R2 / MinIO / Aliyun OSS / Tencent COS / B2, etc.).
settings.imageStorageBackendDesc: After switching to S3, new images go to the object store; existing local files remain readable.
settings.imageS3EndpointDesc: Service URL, e.g. https://{accountid}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com or https://oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com. Leave blank for AWS S3.
settings.imageS3RegionDesc: Region code; R2/MinIO can use auto.
settings.imageS3SecretKeyDesc: Secret key — stored in the database when saved.
settings.imageS3PrefixDesc: Optional; prepended to every object key, e.g. codex/images.
settings.imageS3ForcePathStyleDesc: Required by MinIO; usually off for AWS S3 / R2.
settings.backgroundImageDesc: Set a global admin background. Supports URLs, local paths, uploaded images, or MP4 live wallpapers; images can be up to 20MB, MP4 can be up to 40MB, and background/surface opacity and blur can be tuned separately.
settings.backgroundImageInvalidType: Background media must be PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, or MP4
settings.backgroundImageTooLarge: Background image must be 20MB or smaller
settings.backgroundVideoTooLarge: MP4 live wallpaper must be 40MB or smaller
settings.modelRegistryDesc: Sync the official OpenAI Codex models page and merge it into the local model list.
settings.modelsSyncSuccess: Model sync complete: {{added}} added, {{updated}} updated, {{skipped}} skipped

proxies.noProxiesDesc: Click "Add Proxy" to configure the proxy pool

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.description: Manage the keys downstream clients use to access the /v1/* API.
apiKeys.authDisabledDesc: /v1/* is open until the first key is created
apiKeys.createDesc: Let the system generate an sk-* key, or enter a custom key for existing clients.
apiKeys.quotaInvalid: Quota limit must be a number greater than or equal to 0
apiKeys.expiresAtPast: Expiration time must be in the future
apiKeys.keyCreatedDesc: The new key is expanded in the list for copying or hiding.
apiKeys.keyCreateSuccess: Key created and expanded in the list
apiKeys.deleteKeyDesc: After deletion, all clients using this key will immediately lose access.
apiKeys.keyAuthNote: API requires no auth without keys. After adding the first key, all /v1/* requests need Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx.
apiKeys.tableDesc: Use this list to show, hide, copy, or delete client keys.
apiKeys.noKeysDesc: After creating the first key, client requests will require authentication.
apiKeys.allowedGroupsHint: Leaving this empty allows the key to use all account groups.

limits.title: Advanced limits
limits.desc: Apply per-key model scope and sliding-window quota. Leave a field empty to skip it.
limits.modelAllowPlaceholder: Pick or type a model, press Enter
limits.modelAllowHint: When non-empty, only listed models are allowed; otherwise the denylist applies.
limits.modelDenyPlaceholder: Pick or type a model to deny
limits.modelDenyHint: Used only when the allowlist is empty.

apiKeys.permissionDesc: Restrict this key to specific account groups. Leaving it empty allows all groups.
apiKeys.editDesc: Adjust the name, quota, expiration, and account group permissions. The key value is not changed.
apiKeys.clearExpirationHint: Saving will clear the expiration time and keep this key valid indefinitely.
apiKeys.relativeExpirationHint: Saving will set the key to expire {{days}} days from now.
apiKeys.tokenUsageDesc: Pick a time range to break down requests, tokens, and cost per key.
apiKeys.tokenUsageSearchPlaceholder: Search by key name or fragment

guide.title: Usage Guide
guide.description: Learn how to configure and call CodexProxy API endpoints
guide.endpointsDesc: CodexProxy provides three API endpoints compatible with different client formats.
guide.responsesDesc: Native Codex Responses API endpoint. Directly proxies requests to upstream. For Codex CLI, OpenAI SDK and other native clients.
guide.chatDesc: OpenAI Chat Completions compatible endpoint. Accepts OpenAI format, translates to Codex format, and translates responses back. For ChatGPT compatible clients.
guide.messagesDesc: Anthropic Messages API compatible endpoint. Accepts Claude format, translates to Codex format, and translates responses back. For Claude Code and other Anthropic clients.
guide.codexDesc: Add the following configuration files to the Codex CLI config directory to use Codex models through this proxy.
guide.codexConfigHint: Make sure the following content is at the beginning of config.toml
guide.codexNoteUnix: Make sure the config directory exists. Run mkdir -p ~/.codex to create it.
guide.codexNoteWindows: Press Win+R, type %userprofile%\.codex to open the config directory. Create it manually if it doesn't exist.
guide.claudeCodeDesc: Set environment variables or edit settings.json to let Claude Code use Codex models through this proxy.
guide.claudeCodeNote: Replace YOUR_API_KEY with the API Key created on the API Keys page. Model mapping can be configured in Settings.
guide.claudeEnvNote: These environment variables only apply to the current terminal session. For permanent configuration, add them to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc.
guide.claudeSettingsNote: Or directly edit the Claude Code settings.json config file (choose one method).
guide.authDesc: All endpoints support the following authentication methods (in priority order):
guide.modelMappingDesc: Model mapping is split into Claude mapping and Codex model redirects. Codex mapping supports * wildcard rules such as gpt-*, *-mini, or *codex*.

images.title: Image Studio
images.description: Text-to-image generation, prompt templates, and server gallery

notices.transparent: Transparent backgrounds need PNG or WebP.
notices.highQuality: 4K and High work best on Plus or Team.
notices.accountRouting: Image jobs prefer Plus or higher accounts.

images.autoSizeHint: Auto size omits size so model aliases and prompt ratio rules can apply.
images.jobHistoryHint: Review every image generation job. Failed jobs show the upstream error summary.
images.templateDetailsHint: Maintain name, tags, and usage records for easier reuse.
images.newTemplateHint: Fill in a name, tags, and prompt body, then save it as a new template.
images.templateContentHint: Maintain reusable prompt text and style guidance without generation job state.
images.templateDialogDesc: Maintain the template prompt and image defaults in this dialog, then reuse it from Studio.
images.promptManagerHint: Select a template on the left to edit it. Apply it to Studio when you want to generate an image.
images.galleryHint: Click an image to preview it, then download, copy the prompt, rerun, save as a template, or delete it.
images.deleteJobDesc: Delete job #{{id}} and its associated images. This cannot be undone.
images.inputImageHint: Upload one or more reference images; AI will modify based on these images
images.inputImageRequired: Reference image is required for image-to-image mode

settings2.modelMappingDesc: Configure model mapping rules. Claude mapping and Codex model redirects are maintained separately.
settings2.anthropicModelMappingDesc: Used by /v1/messages to map Claude/Anthropic model names to Codex model names.
settings2.codexModelMappingDesc: Redirect Codex models for Chat, Responses, Messages, and Images, for example gpt-5.2 -> gpt-5.5. The left side supports * wildcards.
settings2.reasoningEffortModelsDesc: Generate requestable model names with reasoning effort for Codex models, for example gpt-5.5(xhigh).

apiRef.title: API Reference
apiRef.description: CodexProxy API endpoint reference documentation with request formats and response examples
apiRef.authDesc: All API requests require a key passed via one of the following methods:

responses.title: Create Response
responses.desc: Create a Codex Responses API response. Supports streaming and non-streaming modes, directly proxied to upstream Codex API.

chat.title: Create Chat Completion
chat.desc: Create an OpenAI Chat Completions format conversation completion. Requests are automatically translated to Codex format, responses translated back to OpenAI format.

messages.title: Create Message
messages.desc: Create an Anthropic Messages API format message. Requests are automatically translated to Codex format, responses translated back to Anthropic format. Model names are mapped according to the mapping table in system settings.

health.title: Health Check
health.desc: Check service status and available account count. This endpoint does not require authentication.

apiRef.accountSectionDesc: The following endpoints are for managing Codex accounts. Requires X-Admin-Key header with admin secret.

addAccount.title: Add Account (Refresh Token)
addAccount.desc: Add a single account via Refresh Token. Automatically refreshes Access Token and joins the account pool. name is optional, proxy_url can be empty.

addAccountBatch.title: Batch Add Accounts (Refresh Token)
addAccountBatch.desc: Batch add accounts: separate multiple tokens with newline \n in the refresh_token field. Maximum 100 per request. Names are auto-suffixed with sequence numbers.

addATAccount.title: Add Account (Access Token)
addATAccount.desc: Add account directly via Access Token (AT-only mode, no Refresh Token needed). Supports batch: separate multiple tokens with newline in access_token.

importAccounts.title: Import Accounts (File Upload)
importAccounts.desc: Batch import accounts via file upload. Supports three formats: txt (one RT per line), json (CLIProxyAPI credential export compatible), at_txt (one AT per line). Max file size 20MB.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Account
deleteAccount.desc: Delete account by ID and remove from the pool.

listAccounts.title: List Accounts
listAccounts.desc: List all accounts with their status, usage and other details.

tryIt.placeholder: Click Send to make a request

docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Complete integration guide, API endpoints and admin reference.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.description: Pick your favorite AI client. Copy config or launch the app in one click.
quickStart.toolsDesc: Curated set of CLI, desktop, web, and mobile tools.
quickStart.curlDesc: Use these commands to verify your endpoints. YOUR_API_KEY auto-fills with your first API key.

clientConfig.title: Client Configuration
clientConfig.description: Wire this proxy into Codex CLI or Claude Code. Covers every supported protocol.
clientConfig.codexDesc: Add the following config files to the Codex CLI config directory to route Codex traffic through this proxy.
clientConfig.ccSwitchPreviewDesc: Confirm the target app, endpoint, and models, then launch CC Switch to import directly.
clientConfig.cherryPreviewDesc: Generate a provider import link from the current endpoint and API key.
clientConfig.codexConfigHint: Make sure the content below appears at the top of config.toml
clientConfig.codexNoteUnix: Make sure the config directory exists — run mkdir -p ~/.codex if not.
clientConfig.codexNoteWindows: Press Win+R and enter %userprofile%\.codex to open the config directory — create it manually if missing.
clientConfig.claudeDesc: Set environment variables or edit settings.json to make Claude Code use this proxy.
clientConfig.claudeEnvNote: Environment variables only persist for the current shell session — write them to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc for permanent use.
clientConfig.claudeSettingsNote: Or edit Claude Code's settings.json directly (pick one approach).
clientConfig.mappingDesc: Model mapping is split into Claude mapping and Codex model redirects. Codex mapping supports * wildcard rules such as gpt-*, *-mini, or *codex*.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.description: All endpoints except /health require an API key. Admin endpoints additionally require X-Admin-Key.

modelApi.title: API Reference
modelApi.description: OpenAI Responses, Chat Completions, and Anthropic Messages — three compatible surfaces sharing one upstream pool.

adminApi.title: Account Management API
adminApi.description: Add, delete, and list pool accounts. All endpoints require X-Admin-Key.
